```{r clustered-se}

# ── Map each model name → the dataset it was fit on ──────────────────────────
# Models prefixed diab_* use diab.subgroup; htn_* use htn.subgroup.
# Both datasets must have a client_nbr column for clustering.

model_data <- list(
  diab = diab.subgroup,
  htn  = htn.subgroup
)

# ── Helper: tidy a model using clustered SEs ─────────────────────────────────
tidy_clustered <- function(model, data, cluster_var = "client_nbr") {

  is_nb <- inherits(model, "negbin")

  # Clustered variance-covariance matrix
  cl_vcov <- sandwich::vcovCL(model, cluster = as.formula(paste0("~", cluster_var)), data = data)

  # coeftest gives us SE / t / p with the new vcov
  ct <- lmtest::coeftest(model, vcov = cl_vcov)

  # Build tidy-style data frame
  coef_df <- as.data.frame(ct[, , drop = FALSE])
  colnames(coef_df) <- c("estimate", "std.error", "statistic", "p.value")
  coef_df$term <- rownames(coef_df)
  rownames(coef_df) <- NULL

  # 95% CIs from the clustered vcov
  ci <- lmtest::coefci(model, vcov = cl_vcov, level = 0.95)
  coef_df$conf.low  <- ci[, 1]
  coef_df$conf.high <- ci[, 2]

  # For negative binomial: exponentiate estimate + CIs (IRR)
  if (is_nb) {
    coef_df <- coef_df %>%
      mutate(
        conf.low  = exp(conf.low),
        conf.high = exp(conf.high),
        estimate  = exp(estimate)
      )
  }

  coef_df %>% mutate(irr = is_nb)
}

# ── Build clustered-SE results data frame ────────────────────────────────────
lm_coefs_clustered_df <- imap_dfr(
  models,
  ~ {
    # pick the right dataset based on model name prefix
    grp  <- if (grepl("^diab_", .y)) "diab" else "htn"
    dat  <- model_data[[grp]]

    tidy_clustered(.x, dat, cluster_var = "client_nbr") %>%
      mutate(model = .y)
  },
  .id = NULL
) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    model, term, any_of("irr"),
    estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value,
    conf.low, conf.high
  ) %>%
  rename(
    std_error = std.error,
    t_value   = statistic,
    p_value   = p.value,
    conf_low  = conf.low,
    conf_high = conf.high
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    group = case_when(
      grepl("^diab_", model) ~ "diab",
      grepl("^htn_",  model) ~ "htn",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    adjustment = case_when(
      grepl("_adj$",   model) ~ "adj",
      grepl("_unadj$", model) ~ "unadj",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    se_type = "clustered"
  )

# Tag the original results for the comparison join
lm_coefs_original <- lm_coefs_df %>% mutate(se_type = "standard")

# ── Side-by-side comparison ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
se_comparison_df <-
  bind_rows(lm_coefs_original, lm_coefs_clustered_df) %>%
  dplyr::select(model, term, se_type, estimate, std_error, p_value, conf_low, conf_high) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = se_type,
    values_from = c(std_error, p_value, conf_low, conf_high)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    se_ratio       = std_error_clustered / std_error_standard,   # >1 means clustering inflated SE
    ci_width_std   = conf_high_standard  - conf_low_standard,
    ci_width_clust = conf_high_clustered - conf_low_clustered
  ) %>%
  arrange(model, term)

```

```{r compare-se-table}

# ── Flextable: main ITS terms only, adjusted models only ─────────────────────
its_terms <- c("t", "period_FFCRA", "period_Exposure_Unwinding",
               "t_after_FFCRA", "t_after_Exposure")

term_labels <- c(
  t                            = "Pre-trend",
  period_FFCRA                 = "Level Change, FFCRA",
  t_after_FFCRA                = "Trend Change, FFCRA",
  period_Exposure_Unwinding    = "Level Change, Exposure Unwinding",
  t_after_Exposure             = "Trend Change, Exposure Unwinding"
)

se_comparison_df %>%
  filter(grepl("_adj$", model), term %in% its_terms) %>%
  mutate(
    term  = recode(term, !!!term_labels),
    model = gsub("_adj$", "", model)   # tidy up label
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    model, term, estimate,
    std_error_standard, std_error_clustered, se_ratio,
    p_value_standard,   p_value_clustered,
    conf_low_standard,  conf_high_standard,
    conf_low_clustered, conf_high_clustered
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 4))) %>%
  flextable() %>%
  set_header_labels(
    model                = "Model",
    term                 = "Term",
    estimate             = "Estimate",
    std_error_standard   = "SE (Standard)",
    std_error_clustered  = "SE (Clustered)",
    se_ratio             = "SE Ratio\n(Clust/Std)",
    p_value_standard     = "p (Standard)",
    p_value_clustered    = "p (Clustered)",
    conf_low_standard    = "CI Low\n(Standard)",
    conf_high_standard   = "CI High\n(Standard)",
    conf_low_clustered   = "CI Low\n(Clustered)",
    conf_high_clustered  = "CI High\n(Clustered)"
  ) %>%
  theme_box() %>%
  bold(part = "header") %>%
  color(
    ~ se_ratio > 1.10,           # highlight rows where clustering meaningfully inflated SE
    j = "se_ratio",
    color = "firebrick"
  ) %>%
  autofit() %>%
  FitFlextableToPage()

```

```{r export-clustered}

# Add clustered results to the Excel export
lm_results_clustered_df <- lm_coefs_clustered_df %>%
  left_join(lm_fit_df, by = "model") %>%
  mutate(state = "texas", measure = "regression_clustered_se", use = "table")

# Append sheets to the existing output file
wb <- openxlsx::loadWorkbook(output_file)
openxlsx::addWorksheet(wb, "lm_results_clustered_se")
openxlsx::addWorksheet(wb, "se_comparison")
openxlsx::writeData(wb, "lm_results_clustered_se", lm_results_clustered_df)
openxlsx::writeData(wb, "se_comparison",            se_comparison_df)
openxlsx::saveWorkbook(wb, output_file, overwrite = TRUE)

```
